title: For the Refugee
scripture_text: The stranger who sojourns with you shall be to you as the native among you, and you shall love him as yourself.
reflection: The people Jesus loved most were the ones in motion. The refugees, the migrants, the ones who had been turned away.
body:
Somewhere tonight, a family is sleeping in a place they did not plan to sleep. They have left everything. The children do not understand. The parents do, too well.

The Lord was once a refugee. He slept in Egypt as a baby. He knows the cold of an unfamiliar country, the strange food, the language that does not belong to you.

He is with them. He is also asking what you will do. The room you have is not just a room. The food in your kitchen is not just food. The vote you cast is not just a vote.

Love your neighbor. He is in the next town, or across the sea. He is the same command.
prayer: Lord, for the refugee, for the migrant, for the one in a strange land tonight. Be their home until they find one. Amen.
